description: The soft seed fibre of cotton plants, whether gathered loose or prepared for textile work, is 'sophae'. Cotton varies in colour, staple length, strength, and treatment. 'linua' (fiber) is the wider material form; 'sophae' keeps the plant source in view.
articulatory_notes: A narrow tongue groove sends unvoiced 's' into rounded 'o'. Unvoiced air then passes between nearly closed lips into stressed 'a', followed by a clean hiatus into final front 'e'.
usage_notes: The phrase 'sophae wetha' (cotton cloth) puts the fibre source first. Provenance or processing can join the account when either changes the claim.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fibre length, moisture, seed removal, and twist change how cotton behaves under the hand. Spinning begins with those particulars, not with the finished cloth imagined in advance.

semantic_domains.creation: a plant fibre spun or assembled into textile material
semantic_domains.nature: seed hairs produced by cotton plants
semantic_domains.physical: a soft cellulose fibre with variable length and treatment
